rulings.Case Title: DIANE SCHOBER et al VS. KAMRAN ARDEBILCHI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Nonparty Airbnb, Inc.'S Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Personal Appearance And Production Of Documents And Things
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 7, 2015, line 1. DEFENDANT AIRBNB, INC.'s Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Personal Appearance And Production Of Documents And Things OFF CALENDAR. This case was assigned to Judge Karnow in Department 304 on March 10, 2015. All pending motions should be refiled with that Department. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL J. SKAAR et al VS. THOMAS CLEMENTS LACEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike And Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 7, 2015, line 2. PLAINTIFFs MICHAEL SKAAR and ROGER COLE's Motion To Strike And Tax Costs DENIED. The early filing of Defendant's Memorandum of Costs is not a reason to strike it. (See Pamela W. v. Millsom (1994) 25 Cal.App.4th 950, 961.) Costs for party depositions are expressly recoverable by statute, and the Court finds the JAMS mediation was also reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation and therefore properly recoverable as a cost. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL J. SKAAR et al VS. THOMAS CLEMENTS LACEY et al

rulings.Case Title: JOHN WONG VS. NICK LANHAM et al
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 7, 2015, line 3. rulings.Case Title: ARCHIBALD CUNNINGHAM VS. KEVIN SINGER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendants Coombs And Woods To Expunge Lis Pendens
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 7, 2015, line 4. DEFENDANTs MICHAEL COOMBS and TAMARA WOODS' Motion Of Defendants Coombs And Woods To Expunge Lis Pendens Plaintiff's peremptory challenge to Judge Quidachay is accepted. Defendants' Motion to Expunge Lis Pendens is transferred to Judge Ulmer in Department 306, to be heard tomorrow, April 7, 2015 at 9:30AM. Judge Ulmer issues the following tentative ruling: Defendants' Motion to Expunge Lis Pendens is GRANTED. Opposing party has not established by a preponderance of the evidence the probable validity of a real property claim. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 405.32.) Plaintiff's claims are an attempt to re-litigate past cases and final judgments in those cases bar his claims; as such, he has no probability of success on the merits. Per statute, a motion to expunge a lis pendens can be brought at any time after the filing of the lis pendens, by any party or non-party. (CCP section 405.30). Plaintiff's contention that Defendants must first answer the complaint is not persuasive or supported by any law. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: TIFFANY OCON VS. XENIA RAMOS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 7, 2015, line 6. Plaintiff's Notice was proper and the Complaint properly states a cause of action. (See SFRO § 37.9(i).). = (501/REQ)
